mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing investors to devalue equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals immediate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending forecasts and equity valuations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with payrolls above 150,000 show negative median 20-day forward returns, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for prior month trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or positive returns dominate when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms macroeconomic expansion, reducing tail-risk premiums and triggering short-term equity buying pressure that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP releases above 2.5% are followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which depresses near-term corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth correlates with positive 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.
